senses.definition: much better than others of the same type

examples.x: Her artwork is truly a cut above; the attention to detail and the mastery of technique set her apart from other artists.
The gourmet restaurant is a cut above the rest; their innovative cuisine and impeccable service make for a memorable dining experience.
The new luxury hotel is a cut above other accommodations in the area; its elegant design and top-notch amenities provide a superior level of comfort.
The athlete's performance was a cut above the competition; her speed, agility, and precision were unmatched.
The company's customer service is a cut above; their prompt and personalized assistance sets them apart from their competitors.

word_list.pronunciation: hˈɛd ænd ʃˈoʊldɚz əbˌʌv ˌɛsbˈiː slˈæʃ ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ

senses.definition: used to describe someone or something that is far superior when compared to others of the same type

examples.x: The talented pianist was head and shoulders above the other competitors in the music competition, showcasing exceptional skill and artistry.
The new smartphone model is head and shoulders above its competitors in terms of camera quality and innovative features.
The skilled surgeon was head and shoulders above her peers, known for her precision and outstanding surgical outcomes.
The young tennis player displayed remarkable talent, standing head and shoulders above others in her age group, and quickly rising through the ranks of the sport.
The new restaurant in town is head and shoulders above the rest when it comes to culinary innovation and unique flavors.

senses.definition: a delicate distinction between two things, particularly two apparently similar situations or concepts

examples.x: There's a fine line between confidence and arrogance; it's important to strike a balance.
The comedian's jokes often tread a thin line between humor and offensiveness, causing mixed reactions from the audience.
In competitive sports, there is a fine line between pushing oneself to the limit and risking injury.
The decision to intervene in someone's personal matters can be a thin line between genuine concern and overstepping boundaries.

senses.definition: a situation in which things are being evaluated or considered in a fair manner

examples.x: When assessing job candidates, it is essential to compare them apples to apples by evaluating their qualifications and experience in relation to the specific requirements of the position.
In order to determine the best investment option, it is crucial to compare the potential returns and risks of different opportunities apples to apples.
When analyzing research studies, scientists carefully examine methodology and sample size to compare findings apples to apples and draw accurate conclusions.
When negotiating a contract, it is important to compare the terms and conditions of different offers apples to apples to ensure a fair and balanced agreement.

word_list.pronunciation: biː kˈʌt fɹʌm ˌaʊɾəv ðə sˈeɪm klˈɔθ

senses.definition: to possses qualities that are very similar to those of someone or something else's

examples.x: Despite coming from very different backgrounds, the scientists found they were cut from the same cloth in their love of discovery and solving complex problems.
The CEO criticized two executives for not being cut from the same cloth, as their management approaches were incompatible.
After years of studying together, we realized we were cut from the same cloth and shared many of the same hopes, beliefs, and values.
A team is never truly cohesive until its members discover they were cut from the same cloth.

senses.definition: used to convey that someone is receiving much less credit or attention compared to a person more popular or skillful
to try to do the exact same things as others did before one
to behave in the same manner as someone else

examples.x: After the successful launch of their new product, other companies quickly followed suit by introducing similar offerings.
When the team captain decided to take a risk, the rest of the team followed suit and embraced the bold strategy.
Seeing their classmates forming study groups, the remaining students decided to follow suit in order to improve their academic performance.
After witnessing the positive impact of flexible work arrangements, many companies started to follow suit and implement similar policies.
When one artist experimented with a new art style and gained recognition, several others in the art community followed suit and explored similar techniques.

word_list.pronunciation: tˈeɪk bˈɔːɹoʊ ɐ lˈiːf ˌaʊɾəv wˈʌnz bˈʊk

senses.definition: to do something or to behave in a manner that is almost identical to that of someone else's

examples.x: After witnessing her friend's dedication to fitness, Sarah decided to take a leaf out of her book and start exercising regularly.
Inspired by the success of a renowned entrepreneur, he decided to borrow a leaf out of her book and implement similar strategies in his own business.
Seeing her colleague's calm and composed demeanor during stressful situations, Jane decided to take a leaf out of his book and work on managing her own stress better.
The young artist admired the unique painting style of a renowned master and decided to borrow a leaf out of his book by incorporating similar techniques into her own artwork.

word_list.pronunciation: tˈeɪk ɐ kjˈuː fɹʌm ˌɛsbˈiː slˈæʃ ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ

senses.definition: to take notice of someone else's action or behavior in an attempt to reach the same success

examples.x: In order to improve his public speaking skills, he took a cue from a renowned orator and studied their delivery techniques.
The team decided to take a cue from their successful competitors and implement similar marketing strategies to enhance their own brand.
When facing a challenging situation, it can be helpful to take a cue from past experiences and apply the lessons learned to navigate the present circumstance.
The fashion designer took a cue from current trends and incorporated them into her latest collection, ensuring it would appeal to contemporary tastes.

word_list.pronunciation: biː bɪkˌʌm hˈæf ðə mˈæn wˈʊmən pˈɜːsən ðæt ˌɛsbˈiː ɪz

senses.definition: to be much less powerful, successful, or capable when compared to someone else

word_list.pronunciation: nˌɑːt ɐ pˈætʃ ˌɑːn ˌɛsbˈiː slˈæʃ ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ

senses.definition: (of a person or thing) greatly inferior to another person or thing

examples.x: The new restaurant in town is not a patch on the established fine-dining establishments in terms of culinary expertise and ambiance.
The sequel to the critically acclaimed film was not a patch on its predecessor, lacking the same depth and originality.
The rookie player's performance was not a patch on the seasoned veteran's skill and experience on the field.
The amateur singer's rendition of the song was not a patch on the professional singer's powerful and captivating performance.

senses.definition: used to refer to two individuals or entities that have the same qualities or having acquired the same achievements

examples.x: The new restaurant in town is not in the same league as the Michelin-starred establishments when it comes to culinary excellence.
Although he is a talented musician, he is not in the same league as the virtuoso pianist who performed last night.
The local team realized they were not in the same league as the national champions, struggling to keep up with their superior skills.
The budget-friendly smartphone cannot be compared to the premium flagship models; they are not in the same league in terms of performance and features.
After witnessing her powerful presentation skills, I realized I am not in the same league as her when it comes to public speaking.

senses.definition: used to suggest that that someone is not on the same level or does not possess the same social standing, attractiveness, or qualifications as another person

examples.x: She's so successful and sophisticated; I feel like she's completely out of my league.
I wanted to ask him out, but he's a famous actor—totally out of my league.
The wealthy entrepreneur was dating someone who worked as a barista; some thought it was out of his league.

senses.definition: (of a person or thing) to be much faster, better, or more successful compared to someone or something else

examples.x: The chess grandmaster ran circles around his opponents, effortlessly defeating them in every match.
The agile gymnast ran rings around her competitors, executing complex routines with precision and grace.
The brilliant student ran circles around his classmates, consistently achieving the highest grades in every subject.
The experienced negotiator ran rings around the inexperienced salesperson, easily securing a more favorable deal.

senses.definition: used to compare two things based on their qualities or abilities, without considering their size or scale

examples.x: Pound for pound, this restaurant offers the best value for money in the city.
Pound for pound, the small boutique produces the most exquisite handmade jewelry in the region.
The small coffee shop may not have the grandeur of a large chain, but pound for pound, it serves the best coffee in town.
The compact car may not have the same horsepower as a sports car, but pound for pound, it offers impressive fuel efficiency.
The novel may be short in length, but pound for pound, it delivers a powerful and thought-provoking story.

senses.definition: a vast difference between two things, often indicating that they are significantly distant or dissimilar in terms of progress, quality, or understanding

examples.x: Her knowledge of advanced mathematics is light years away from mine; I struggle with basic calculations.
The quality of their customer service is light years away from what we experienced with the previous company.
In terms of artistic talent, his paintings are light years away from anything I could create.
The technology used in their new smartphone is light years away from the outdated model I have.
The gap in experience between the two teams was evident; one was light years away from the other in terms of skill and strategy.
